description: baseline hrm-G replay: lognormal frozen scales + frozen non-embed FP, Lion-on-embed, Bop Bet1 tau_norm=0.15
cautious mask on BopTernary (sign(m)==sign(g_t)); rest = baseline
CMuon (Newton-Schulz5 orthogonalized momentum + cautious mask) on STE'd ternary latents; non-trit FP via Lion; rest = baseline structure
SCREENING s1: Bop+cautious on tiny non-loop (26M, H=L=2, cycles=1x1, 1500 steps)
SCREENING s2: CMuon STE on tiny non-loop (vs s1=5.4674, -0.28)
SCREENING s3: Lion-STE on tiny non-loop (5.5221, worse than s2 CMuon 5.1850 and s1 Bop+cautious 5.4674)
SCREENING s4: CMuon-STE + int8 per-token-absmax activations. val 5.1848 vs s2 5.1850 (noise-level, int8 act is free)
SCREENING s5: CMuon-STE + fp16 m state. val 5.1962 vs s2 5.1850 (+0.011). Small but real penalty; revisit if memory pressure on main run.
MAIN HRM 153M + CMuon-STE + int8 act, INTERRUPTED by user at step 475. int8 act tax was +50% wall time on main (12.2 s/step vs Run 3's 8.1) — not worth it for fastest-recipe metric. Discard.
FAST-A baseline: 38M HRM (hidden=512, H=L=2, cycles=2x3), CMuon-STE. val 6.5510 (vs Run 3 full 153M 6.5320, only +0.019). Validates fast-A as iteration surrogate.

FAST-A + full BPTT first 500 steps then 1-step. FINAL val 6.5714 > baseline 6.5510 (discard by metric) BUT val at end of full-BPTT phase (step 500) was 6.2078 — drastically better. The 1-step phase visibly DEGRADED the model. Suggests full BPTT throughout will dominate.

FAST-A + FULL BPTT throughout 2500 steps. val 4.9572 — improvement of -1.59 vs Run 10 (6.5510). The 1-step gradient approximation was THE bottleneck on HRM training in our setup. New champion by a huge margin.

MAIN 153M HRM + CMuon-STE + full BPTT throughout, bs=1 ga=32. val 4.7779 — new champion, -1.75 vs Run 3 (prior 153M champion), -2.13 vs baseline Run 1. Confirms full BPTT recipe scales from 38M to 153M.
FAST-A + full BPTT + NON-cautious CMuon. val 5.0628 vs Run 12's 4.9572 (+0.11). Cautious mask IS helping at 90% zeroing rate on HRM; keep cautious=True.
FAST-A + full BPTT + CMuon lr=0.01 (half). val 5.1950 vs Run 12's 4.9572 (+0.24). Lower LR hurts. Try higher.
FAST-A + full BPTT + CMuon lr=0.05 (2.5x Run 12's 0.02). val 4.5923 — new best, beats Run 13 (153M lr=0.02) at 4.78! Higher LR is the right direction.

FAST-A + last-per-cycle grad-mode + lr=0.05. Interrupted at step 2050 but val trajectory was clearly losing decisively to Run 16's full BPTT (5.96 vs 4.59). Last-per-cycle gives ~20% wall savings but huge quality cost.
FAST-A + full BPTT + CMuon lr=0.10 (2x Run 16's 0.05). val 4.5001 — new champion, -0.09 vs Run 16. LR sweep diminishing returns visible.
FAST-A + full BPTT + CMuon lr=0.20. val 4.5808 (+0.08 vs Run 18's 4.50). LR peak found at 0.10.
MAIN 153M + full BPTT + CMuon lr=0.10 + cautious. val 4.2905 — NEW ALL-TIME CHAMPION. -0.49 vs Run 13 (lr=0.02), -2.62 vs baseline. Confirms lr=0.10 scales from fast-A.
FAST-A + full BPTT + CMuon lr=0.10 with cosine decay to 0.01. val 4.4436 — -0.057 vs Run 18 4.50. Cosine schedule on CMuon helps modestly.
FAST-A + CMuon lr=0.20 cosine to 0.02. val 4.3607 — best fast-A, -0.08 vs Run 21 (lr=0.10 cosine), -0.14 vs Run 18 (lr=0.10 constant). High peak + cosine wins.
FAST-A + CMuon lr=0.40 cosine to 0.04. val 4.3566 — tied with Run 22 (4.3607) within noise. LR/cosine plateau around 0.20-0.40.
FAST-A + CMuon lr=0.20 cosine + warmup 200. val 4.3638 vs Run 22 4.3607 (no warmup). Warmup is noise-level neutral. Recipe converged.

MAIN 153M + full BPTT + CMuon lr=0.20 cosine to 0.02. val 4.1562 — NEW ALL-TIME CHAMPION. -0.134 vs Run 20 (lr=0.10 constant), -2.75 vs baseline (-39.8 pct). 15.8 pct trits flipped from zero — most trit motion of any run.
FAST-A + cycles 1x3 (no outer recurrence) + CMuon lr=0.20 cosine. val 4.3479 vs Run 22 (cycles 2x3) 4.3607 — tied within noise, 18 pct faster (5517s vs 6738s). Outer H cycle redundant!
FAST-A + cycles 1x1 (NO recurrence at all). val 4.3524 vs Run 26 (1x3) 4.3479 - tied. 34 pct faster than Run 22 (2x3) at 4464s vs 6738s. Recurrence not earning compute at 2500-step horizon.
FAST-A + cycles 2x3 for 5000 STEPS + lr=0.20 cosine. val 4.1706 — matches Run 25 main 153M! per_loop_gap=0.3916 (12x Run 25 at 2500 steps). User hypothesis confirmed: recurrence pays off LATE.

FAST-A + cycles 1x1 + 5000 steps. val 4.1783 vs Run 28 (2x3 same) 4.1706 — tied within noise, 53 pct FASTER (8131s vs 17380s). Even with per_loop_gap=0.39 in 2x3 showing real recurrence use, it converts to <0.01 nat advantage. 1x1 wins.

metrics.reason: User pivot to variable-loop recurrence research

description: MAIN 153M + 1x1 + 5000 steps. INTERRUPTED at step 200 to pivot research direction to variable-loop fixpoint regularization.

FAST-A + variable H_cycles in [1,4] each step + 5000 steps. val 4.1863 — matches fixed runs (Run 28 2x3 4.17, Run 29 1x1 4.18). KEY: per_loop_gap=0.0037 vs Run 28 0.39 — 100x lower. Model converged to TRUE FIXED-POINT. Recurrence behaves as iterative refinement as HRM theory predicts.

FAST-A + variable H_cycles in [1,8] (wider) + 5000 steps. val 4.1910 — tied with Run 31 [1,4] (4.1863) and fixed runs (Run 28/29 ~4.17) within noise. per_loop_gap=0.0164: ~24x below fixed 2x3 (0.39), so the fixpoint property is ROBUST to a 2x wider loop range. BUT gap is 4x larger than [1,4]'s 0.0037 AND wall=30463s (+56pct vs [1,4] 19522s) since [1,8] averages 4.5 H-cycles/step vs 2.5. Confirmatory, not better: [1,4] is the sweet spot for cleanest fixpoint at least cost.

FAST-A var H_cycles [1,4] + test-time cycle sweep. val 4.1873 (=Run 31/32, recipe unchanged). KEY RESULT (test-time loop extrapolation): val is FLAT to +/-0.0002 from cyc2..cyc24 — a TRUE stable fixed point, stable to 6x the training max (24 vs [1,4]). cyc1=4.1989 (one iter under-converged). NO test-time compute scaling: extra loops hold loss, don't lower it. cyc32=10.8125=ln(V): collapse to degenerate constant fixed point (attention over-smoothing over ~96 stack-apps). So ternary recurrence forms a clean wide-basin fixpoint (not brittle) but is stable-not-improving and over-smooths past ~24 loops.

FP-WEIGHTS CONTROL: mirrors r033 EXACTLY but --fp-weights (CMuon on raw FP weight, no STE/quantize/scales). val 6.3229 — much worse than ternary r033 4.1873 (+2.14 nats). Cycle sweep IDENTICAL shape to ternary: flat fixpoint c2..c12, collapse c16=10.8125, NaN by c48. NOT a clean precision verdict yet: lr=0.20 was tuned for ternary latents in [-1,1] and looks too hot for LeCun-scale FP (step-500 val 6.86 already underwater). Per ideas#G: sweep FP CMuon lr (0.05, 0.02, 0.01) before concluding. Discard from leaderboard (control line, not on ternary metric).

FP CMuon-LR sweep leg 1: lr=0.05 cosine. val 5.5042 — HUGE improvement vs r034 lr=0.20 (6.3229, -0.82 nats), confirming lr was the main issue. Still +1.32 nats behind ternary r033 4.1873. Stable fixpoint widened to c2..c16 (vs r034 c2..c12, collapse pushed from c16→c24). Trajectory still descending steeply at step 5000 (cosine floor), so FP optimum not yet found. flip_rate=0 because trits frozen via --freeze-scales but quantize disabled by --fp-weights. Off ternary leaderboard (control line).

FP CMuon-LR sweep leg 2 (bisect): lr=0.10 cosine. val 5.9207 — worse than r035 lr=0.05 (5.5042) by +0.42. Confirms FP optimum is at-or-below 0.05, NOT bracketed by 0.05-0.20. Monotone trend r034(6.32)→r036(5.92)→r035(5.50). Stable cycle range c2..c16 same as r035. First run had transient cudaErrorLaunchFailure at step ~50 (driver glitch, same config worked on retry). Off ternary leaderboard.

FP CMuon-LR sweep leg 3: lr=0.02 cosine. val 4.1575 — BEATS ternary r033 (4.1873) by 0.030 nats. Sweep trend: 6.32(0.20)→5.92(0.10)→5.50(0.05)→4.16(0.02). Per-loop gap 0.0185 vs ternary 0.0049 (looser fixpoint but still 1/20 of fixed-cycle). Stable cycle range c2..c16, collapse c24 (same shape as r035). Trajectory still descending step 4500→5000 (-0.013). Answers ideas#F definitively: FP and ternary BOTH form a fixpoint at this scale, FP slightly wins at its own optimum LR. So recurrence is NOT brittle because of ternary; the ~0.03-nat ternary deficit is a true precision tax, not a recurrence-vs-precision artefact. Off ternary leaderboard (FP control line) but biggest data point of the sweep.
